notes: F1/F2/F3/F4/F6 filter logic added to bsip0_nutrition.py (TASK-211).
Runner delegates to shared module so this test also validates exports.
No filter bugs found: all 13 cases passed without code changes.
F6-001: kJ mismatch correctly caught by detect_kj_energy_misparse.
F4 dedup: completeness-ranked sort correctly selects richer record.

results.description: Product missing energy, protein, and sodium (3 of 6 core nutrients absent) — should be rejected as nutritionally incomplete
Product with only energy present — all other core fields absent — catastrophic incompleteness from Carrefour scrape miss
Product with ingredients field null — cannot compute NOVA or additive score
Product with ingredients set to 'ראה אריזה' (see packaging) — non-informative placeholder, should be filtered
Product with ingredients set to empty string — should be filtered like null
Baby wipes product scraped from Rami Levy multi-category shelf — no nutrition, no ingredients, name contains non-food signals
results.filter_reason: non_food: zero nutrition + no ingredients + subcategory='baby_care'

results.description: Cleaning product (dish soap) scraped from mixed shelf — no nutrition table at all
results.filter_reason: non_food: zero nutrition + no ingredients + subcategory='cleaning'

results.description: Corpus with two records sharing barcode 7290000888004: first is complete (all 6 nutrients), second is incomplete (missing energy and protein). Dedup should keep the complete record.
Corpus with two records sharing barcode 7290000999005: both complete but one has more non-null fields (has fiber and sugar; other doesn't). Dedup should keep the richer record.

Worst-case product: max sugar (55g), max sodium (1800mg), max saturated fat (35g out of 40g total fat), min fiber (0), min protein (0.5g), NOVA=4. All pre-score signals should be at worst values. Verifies BSIP0 signals are correct inputs for a guaranteed low score.
Implausibility trap: product declares only 0.3g total fat but 730 kcal (energy consistent with 82g fat). nutrition_implausible should catch this as fat_understated signature.
results.implausible_reason: fat_understated: total_fat=0.3g but declared 730.0kcal exceeds protein+carb+fat energy (8kcal) by 722kcal

results.description: EU-style Carrefour record: energy declared in kJ only (3,138 kJ, which is ~750 kcal for butter). Parser must not use 3138 as if it were kcal — would produce an absurd 3138 kcal/100g score.
results.kj_reason: energy_unit_kj_misread_as_kcal: raw='3138 kJ' parsed to 3138 which exceeds plausible kcal ceiling 900. Likely kJ value (3138 kJ ≈ 750 kcal).

results.description: Carrefour real record with fully empty nutrition (off_miss) — all nutrition fields blank. This is the actual observed Carrefour pattern. The parser must not crash or produce plausible-looking zeros; it must flag as incomplete.
